data.title: Arzopa to unveil new portable displays and digital frames at IFA 2025, showcasing innovative tech.
data.description: A leading global digital display technology company, Arzopa, is set to showcase a range of new products at IFA 2025 in New York on August 29. The lineup demonstrates the brand's commitment to being a top-tier expert in portable imaging, emphasizing premium quality, innovation, and reliability. The company's participation at the event underscores its dedication to staying at the forefront of technological advancements. Visitors can look forward to experiencing firsthand Arzopa's latest offerings designed to enhance digital display experiences.

data.title: SEGG Media reveals revamped website at seggmediacorp.com post turnaround, begins growth phase with sports, entertainment, and gaming assets.
data.description: Fort Worth, TX - On August 29, 2025, SEGG Media Corporation revealed the launch of their revamped corporate website, seggmediacorp.com. The NASDAQ-listed (SEGG, LTRYW) sports, entertainment, and gaming conglomerate unveiled the new site, which marks the completion of their turnaround effort and the start of a vigorous growth stage. Owner of popular platforms like Sports.com, Concerts.com, and Lottery.com, the Company aims to solidify its presence in the digital space with this dynamic hub.

data.title: CoinShares AUM rises by 26% to $3.46B in Q2, profits reach $32.4M. Plans US listing amid Bitcoin and Ether surge.
data.description: In the second quarter, CoinShares reported a profit of $32.4 million and has announced intentions for a listing in the United States. Executives are optimistic about the momentum in the latter half of the year, especially with the surging values of Bitcoin and Ether reaching new highs.

data.title: Theon International Plc to hold Capital Markets Day on Nov 6 in Athens to showcase "THEON Next" Vision & Strategy. Email cmd@theon.com for event registration. More details on Benzinga.com.
data.description: On 29 August 2025, Theon International Plc (THEON) announced details of its upcoming Capital Markets Day (CMD) where it will introduce its vision and strategy for future growth, known as "THEON Next." The company aims to solidify its position as a market leader in optronics and expand globally in modern warfare. The CMD is scheduled for 6 November 2025 in Athens, Greece, at Divani Apollon Palace & Thalasso at 3:00 pm local time. Executives, Leadership Team, and Design & Development Team members will host the event for registered investment professionals, both in-person and virtually. Interested individuals can inquire about registration by contacting cmd@theon.com. Following the CMD, all presentations, materials, and event recordings will be accessible on THEON's website. THEON has already established itself as a frontrunner in... For more information, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Syntec Optics (Nasdaq: OPTX) appoints decorated veteran Louie Salinas as Assistant Director for Military Sales.
data.description: Today, in Rochester, New York, Syntec Optics Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: OPTX), a prominent technology products supplier to defense, biomedical, communications, and consumer sectors, revealed the appointment of Louie Salinas as the Assistant Director for Strategic Military Sales & Customer Development. Louie Salinas will be responsible for driving military sales strategy and customer relationships for the company.

data.title: ENS price declines by 4.76% to $23.19, indicating potential downside as it tests key support.
data.description: The price of ENS has decreased to $23.19 amidst a bearish trend, as technical indicators suggest the possibility of further decline. Ethereum Name Service is nearing important support levels, signaling potential challenges ahead for the cryptocurrency.
BANK OF MEXICO FORECASTS AVERAGE ANNUAL CORE INFLATION IN Q4 2026 AT 3.0% VERSUS PREVIOUS ESTIMATE OF 3.0%

data.title: BYD, China's EV giant, sees net income drop by 30%, below Q2 views, first YOY decline since Q1 2022, stock selling off.
data.description: China's leading electric vehicle company, BYD, experienced a significant 30% decrease in net income, which fell well below second-quarter expectations. This marks the first year-over-year decline for the Tesla competitor since the first quarter of 2022. Consequently, BYD's stock is witnessing a sell-off following the disappointing earnings report.

data.title: "China Construction Market to reach $8.60 Trillion by 2034, grew at 6.00% CAGR from $4.80 Trillion in 2024."
data.description: A report on the China construction market size and share outlook for the period of 2025-2034 has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. In 2024, the China construction market size hit around USD 4.80 trillion. It is anticipated to exhibit a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 6.00% from 2025 to 2034. As such, the market is expected to expand to approximately USD 8.60 trillion by 2034. Construction pertains to the creation of physical structures, buildings, infrastructure, and more.

data.title: Nvidia stock rises 2%, nearing all-time high, while oil prices hit $65/barrel. Wall Street pauses after Fed comments.
data.description: Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ: NVDA) saw a 2% increase in midday trading on Monday, putting it close to its record high and now less than 2% away. Investors are optimistic before the highly anticipated earnings release later in the week. Despite a slight market downturn post-Fed rally on Friday, investors are taking a breather after a strong upward movement, driven by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's comments hinting at a September rate cut. Additionally, crude oil prices surged 2%, surpassing $65 a barrel for the fourth consecutive day, sparking new inflation worries amid expectations of relaxed financial conditions. Geopolitical tensions are once again a concern, amidst hopes for diplomatic efforts. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Otis CEO Judy Marks will speak at Morgan Stanley's 13th annual Laguna conference on September 12, 2025 at 10:00 a.m. PT.
data.description: The Chair, CEO & President of Otis Worldwide Corporation (NYSE: OTIS), Judy Marks, is scheduled to take part in a fireside chat at the 13th annual Laguna conference organized by Morgan Stanley on September 12, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. PT (1:00 p.m. ET) in Farmington, Connecticut.

data.title: US bankruptcies hit record numbers in 2025, with 446 large companies folding. 71 filings in July alone, surpassing 2020. Rates remain high, impacting small caps. Major brands like Forever 21 and Rite Aid have shut down. LifeScan Global, Genesis Healthcare, & Del Monte Foods filed for bankruptcy in July, with over $1 billion in assets and liabilities. Higher for longer rates blamed for the wave.
data.description: Bankruptcies in the U.S. have surged to unprecedented levels, with 446 large companies folding in the first seven months of 2025. This figure marks a 12% increase from the peak of the 2020 pandemic wave and is the highest recorded since 2010. July alone accounted for 71 bankruptcies, the most in a single month since the onset of the COVID-19 crisis. Iconic brands that once thrived in the 1990s, including Forever 21, Joann's, Rite Aid, Party City, and Claire's, have succumbed to closure in 2025. In July, three major names—LifeScan Global, Genesis Healthcare, and Del Monte Foods' second holding company—filed for bankruptcy, each with over $1 billion in assets and liabilities. This rise in bankruptcies is primarily attributed to the persistence of high interest rates in the current "higher for longer" environment, which has particularly impacted small caps. The latest data shows that in the Russell 2000, 43% of companies are operating at a loss, the highest proportion since 2020.

data.title: S&P 500 holds steady awaiting Nvidia results - NVDA stock has doubled since April, with S&P 500 near record highs.
data.description: Wall Street remained cautious on Wednesday with major indices holding steady at midday as investors eagerly anticipated NVIDIA Corp.'s (NASDAQ: NVDA) second-quarter earnings report. NVDA has been outperforming its competitors, having more than doubled in value since hitting a low in April due to tariffs. The stock is seen as a key player in the recent rally that propelled the S&P 500 to record highs. The focus is on Nvidia's Blackwell chip performance, updates from China, and gross margins, which are anticipated to influence both the stock and the broader market. By midday in New York, the S&P 500 rose 0.2% to 6,478, near its record high of 6,481. Nvidia shares gained 0.1% to $182. The Nasdaq 100 also increased by 0.1% to 23,550, standing roughly 2% below its peak, while the Dow climbed 0.3% to 45,500, nearing its own record level. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: NTEC completes 1st residential solar installation on Navajo Nation; funded by NTEC and DOE grant. Over 13,000 homes lack grid connection.
data.description: In Farmington, New Mexico, Navajo Transitional Energy Company (NTEC) has revealed the initiation of 125 solar panel installations in homes on the Navajo Nation. The funding for this project comes from NTEC and a grant from the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations (OCED). The first installation was completed on a remote hogan in the Oljato Chapter of the Navajo Nation. As of now, over 13,000 homes in the Navajo Nation lack access to the electric grid, leading to difficulties in obtaining sufficient water and other necessities.

data.title: Jacobio Pharma reports 2025 interim results with RMB45.7M revenue, RMB93.2M R&D expenses, and RMB59.0M net loss. Maintains RMB1.07B cash.
data.description: Jacobio Pharma (1167.HK) revealed its financial performance for the first half of 2025 today. The company reported revenue of RMB45.7 million for the period, marking a 100% surge from the same timeframe in 2024. Research and development expenses stood at RMB93.2 million. The net loss for the initial half of 2025 was RMB59.0 million, showing a 65.1% reduction year-over-year. As of June 30, 2025, Jacobio held cash, bank balances, and investments totaling RMB1.07 billion, with an additional RMB270 million available in bank credit to support R&D operations. Dr. Yinxiang Wang, the Chairman and CEO, highlighted the milestone of obtaining approval and launching Glecirasib in China in the first half of 2025 through strategic partnerships.

data.title: ICP price plunges by 3.83% to $4.92 due to protocol upgrade delays, testing key support levels and bearish indicators.
data.description: The price of ICP has recently decreased by 3.83% to $4.92. This drop comes as developers express worry over delays in the protocol upgrade. The cryptocurrency is currently testing important support levels, characterized by bearish momentum indicators.

data.title: Actelis Networks reorganizing operations to cut costs by 20% quarterly by 2025, utilizing AI, offshoring, outsourcing.
data.description: The company is strategizing to enhance its operations by leveraging AI, offshoring, and outsourcing to boost flexibility in providing products and services. This includes focusing on reinforcing sales in lucrative sectors and areas. The aim is to achieve a noteworthy 20% decrease in quarterly expenses by the conclusion of 2025.

data.title: Q-Gold secures $10M funding for Quartz Mountain Gold project in Oregon & Mine Centre Camp in Ontario, Canada acquisition.
data.description: The local government announced today that a total of 500 new COVID-19 cases have been reported in the city, bringing the total number of confirmed cases to 10,000. Additionally, there have been 20 new deaths due to the virus, increasing the death toll to 250. The authorities continue to emphasize the importance of following safety guidelines to curb the spread of the virus.

data.title: Dow Jones index barely rises with Palantir stock bouncing back after 15% drop, Nasdaq improves. Fed minutes awaited.
data.description: Ahead of the release of the Federal Reserve meeting minutes, the Dow Jones index showed fluctuations. In trading on Wednesday, Palantir stock experienced a sell-off. Despite this, the stock market today saw the Dow Jones managing to eke out a gain, while the Nasdaq improved from its lowest points. Palantir was able to regain a key level after enduring a significant 15% plunge.

data.title: Apple stock falls due to lag in AI trend. Concerns on company's strategy. Can iPhone maker rebound?
data.description: The stock of Apple has taken a hit due to its significant delay in adopting the artificial intelligence trend, causing concerns among investors. The tech giant's slow progress in AI has led to a decline in its stock value. Investors on Wall Street are worried about Apple's 'invisible' AI strategy and question if the company can reverse the situation. This setback has affected Apple's stock performance significantly.
